name: No Poverty
description: Extreme poverty has fallen from nearly 10% to 8.5% of the global population, but progress has slowed and the pandemic reversed years of gains.

name: Zero Hunger
description: Hunger is rising after decades of decline. Nearly 10% of the world's population faces chronic undernourishment.

name: Good Health and Well-Being
description: Child mortality has fallen to historic lows and life expectancy continues to rise, but universal health coverage remains elusive.

name: Quality Education
description: Global literacy has reached 87% and primary enrollment is near universal, but learning outcomes remain deeply unequal.

name: Gender Equality
description: Girls' education has improved dramatically, but legal rights, political representation, and economic participation still lag far behind.

name: Clean Water and Sanitation
description: Billions have gained access to safe drinking water since 2000, but 2 billion still lack basic sanitation services.

name: Affordable and Clean Energy
description: Access to electricity has expanded to 90% of the world, but renewable energy adoption must accelerate dramatically to meet climate targets.

name: Decent Work and Economic Growth
description: The global economy has grown fivefold since 1950, but productivity gains have not been shared equitably and decent work deficits persist.

name: Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure
description: Internet access and mobile connectivity have transformed the developing world, but the digital divide remains deep.

name: Reduced Inequalities
description: Inequality within and between countries remains stubbornly high. The pandemic widened the gap between richest and poorest.

name: Sustainable Cities and Communities
description: Urbanization continues at an unprecedented pace. More than 1 billion people live in informal settlements without adequate housing.

name: Responsible Consumption and Production
description: Global material footprint continues to rise. The circular economy remains an aspiration rather than a reality.

name: Climate Action
description: Global CO2 emissions continue to rise, now exceeding 37 billion tonnes annually. The window to limit warming to 1.5°C is closing rapidly.

name: Life Below Water
description: Ocean acidification, overfishing, and plastic pollution threaten marine ecosystems that sustain billions of people.

name: Life on Land
description: Deforestation continues at an alarming rate. Biodiversity loss threatens the ecological systems upon which civilization depends.

name: Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions
description: Global peace has deteriorated since 2010. Access to justice and accountable institutions remain distant goals for many.

name: Partnerships for the Goals
description: Development financing and international cooperation must be strengthened significantly to meet the 2030 deadline.
